title: Rustic Venezuelan Corn Flatbread
description: A traditional Venezuelan dish featuring strong white bread flour and a hint of citrus, perfect for a romantic evening. This recipe is sugar free friendly and uses a comal as the cooking vessel.
instructions: Preheat the comal over medium heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cornmeal, salt, and baking powder.
Add the lime juice, olive oil, and water to the bowl and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a flatbread on the preheated comal and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until lightly browned.
Brush the flatbreads with a little water and cook for an additional 30 seconds on each side.
